flags.concern: Test coverage: The plan misses an existing assertion that will fail as soon as `task_id` changes from "REVIEW" to "REVIEW-<check_id>".
flags.evidence: `tests/test_handle_review_robustness.py:364` currently asserts `item["task_id"] == "REVIEW"` for the heavy review integration path, while `_synthesize_review_rework_items()` hard-codes that value at `megaplan/handlers.py:1247`. The plan only budgets the four status tests plus new cases, so without updating this existing integration assertion the requested full pytest command will fail.

flags.concern: Forward compatibility: The proposed unknown-check fallback test does not exercise the real unknown-id path, because `_synthesize_review_rework_items()` drops checks before `expected` is computed when `review_checks.get_check_by_id(check_id)` returns nothing.
flags.evidence: At `megaplan/handlers.py:1223-1225`, any check id missing from `review_checks` is filtered out by the `default_severity` gate before the code reaches `expected` on `megaplan/handlers.py:1249`. Monkeypatching `_EXPECTED_BY_CHECK_ID` with a known check id would only test a missing template entry, not the brief's requested `future_check` fallback behavior.
